4ac8faqkidrhqhtuh0960beklq.summary: Prepare for Praveena's mid-year review by targeting the July 31st deadline—specifically her career trajectory following her Wharton executive program enrollment—while navigating the massive organizational disruptions impacting leadership. Address outstanding transition items—including the AI requirements tool handover to Brian and Steve Ferato's coverage of Auto—to balance team capacity under the current hiring freeze. No work notes match the round-robin tech procurement or the TVO Stacy Clark meeting—these must be addressed as new items today—but you must use this session to align on executive exposure opportunities.
tasks: Finalize the formal mid-year performance review with Praveena before the July 31st deadline.
Map out executive visibility opportunities—such as EMG engage sessions—to leverage her Wharton program training.
Define the objectives for the Stacy Clark TVO meeting and the round-robin tech procurement since no historical notes exist for these topics.
Schedule dedicated syncs with Ray and Steve to manage succession planning and the Auto workload transition.
4ac8faqkidrhqhtuh0960beklq.title: Praveena 1on1 (Mid-Year)

3piuqiulretlc0p34vf262k27i.summary: You have no specific work notes documenting a past meeting with Geoff Troidl—though a July 15 Tech Fellow stand-up note reveals a "Jeff" was abruptly removed from employee roles, triggering an emergency meeting with the CTO. Reviewing your drive files shows active architectural initiatives—specifically USAA two-way texting, IBM's proposed testing engagement, and the SSH Key Management project. Prepare for this follow-up by clarifying Geoff's organizational status and aligning on these critical engineering tracks.
tasks: Confirm Geoff Troidl's current organizational status following the July 15 emergency leadership changes.
Review the pending 250 million dollar IBM testing proposal to address the architecture team's concerns regarding undefined use cases.
Establish a validation plan for the Guidewire pre-production testing environment to replace the proposed manual verification of 65,000 members.
Determine the deployment timeline for the Wiz migration to achieve full parity off Palo Alto products by the December 31, 2026 deadline.
3piuqiulretlc0p34vf262k27i.title: Architecture Follow-up with Geoff Troidl

1ahns5rjkuusbg8sp7s60rvgu8.summary: Your past week of work notes reveals severe organizational shifts—including a strict hiring freeze and the sudden removal of key leaders Jeff and Deepak—alongside critical technical friction points. You must navigate massive vendor decisions—specifically pushing back on a $250 million IBM testing proposal and migrating to Wiz for cloud security—while keeping your team aligned on strict security protocols for Guidewire testing. Share the pending shifts in conversational AI strategy and the pushback on AWS production deployment tracks during your staff meeting to ensure operational alignment.
tasks: Share the critical leadership departures of Jeff and Deepak—and the resulting organizational freeze—with your team immediately.
Halt any unmasked data testing in the Guidewire pre-production environment to enforce strict ISO and NIST security standards.
Brief your team on the selection of Wiz as the new strategic cloud security platform—and the scheduled retirement of Prisma Cloud.
Prepare the technical case to formally oppose the $250 million IBM testing proposal at the next executive review.
Align with Charlotte and Ramesh to resolve the integration friction surrounding the AWS Track 3 production deployment.
1ahns5rjkuusbg8sp7s60rvgu8.title: Weekly Staff

b2og3ns94ou0aoq3gbeamhdra4.summary: You must coordinate cross-portfolio alignment on several front-burner initiatives—including the recent P&C capacity updates and upcoming 2026 infrastructure compliance mandates—while enforcing the new platform-first spending rules across your teams. You need to brief staff on rejecting redundant tools like Brevity in favor of enterprise standards like Zenerate to optimize tech spend. No work notes exist regarding SmartBear—meaning you must initiate a baseline discussion on how this testing suite fits into current modernization efforts.
tasks: Direct your teams to prioritize existing enterprise software licenses over niche products in compliance with the new CIO spending mandate.
Deliver updates to staff on key horizontal alignment items—specifically the upcoming 2026 AMSTACK compliance requirements.
Block the acquisition of duplicate software tools by steering the agency team toward Zenerate instead of Brevity.
Initiate a discovery effort regarding SmartBear usage and licensing to determine its alignment with the enterprise platform-first directive.
b2og3ns94ou0aoq3gbeamhdra4.title: Monthly: LOB Arch Cross-Portfolio Sync
